Gunshot Detection

Can a $3 circuit detect gunshots at 10 meters?

YES.

Ephraim, Kian, Aiden | ENEB304 Microelectronics & Sensors | Spring 2026

The Solution

How It Works

1. Microphone converts sound to voltage

2. Coupling capacitor blocks DC

3. BJT amplifies signal ~400x

4. Oscilloscope captures the output

Total Cost: ~$3

Mic: $1.50 | Transistor: $0.30 | Passives: $0.50

Results

TestPeakFreq
Far (10m)1280 mV2.3 kHz
Medium (5m)1880 mV6.9 kHz
Cap Coupled1200 mV20 kHz

The circuit detects gunshots reliably at 10+ meters with a single amplifier stage.

The Bottom Line

A $3 circuit can detect gunshots at 10+ meters.

Single BJT stage provides ~400x voltage gain.

Frequency analysis reveals 2-20 kHz energy content.

Next: add filtering and threshold detection for a complete low-cost security sensor.

Ephraim, Kian, Aiden | Dr. Nestor Michael C. Tiglao
